name: Nathan A. Lucy
headline: Applied AI systems engineering.
summary: I build production-grade AI systems: agent architecture, MCP-style tool interfaces, custom retrieval, evaluation loops, forecasting systems, data pipelines, and model-facing applications people can actually operate. My background in psychology, premedical biochemistry, and cognition keeps the work grounded in how people think, decide, and trust systems.
strengths: Agent architecture, tool use, and context-aware LLM systems
Custom retrieval, vector-native applications, and evaluation frameworks
AI/ML APIs for training, inference, forecasting, and autonomous insight
Data pipelines from backend services through Snowflake, Sigma BI, and custom UI
Quantitative modeling, separability metrics, and confidence scoring
Clear technical writing, debugging, and architecture review across unfamiliar systems

experience.title: GenAI Systems Developer
bullets: Building custom LLM tools and context-aware systems for enterprise clients.
Focused on shipping generative AI applications with measurable behavior, custom retrieval, and production-grade safety constraints.
Applies Performance-Driven Development principles around representative tasks, evaluation frameworks, latency, cost, robustness, and measurable progress toward production trust.

experience.title: Founder & AI Engineer
bullets: Developed MarketMaven, a vector-native finance intelligence system that applies ideas from fluid dynamics to market structure and breakout analysis.
Built LLM-supported public sentiment analysis qualified against deep embedding-space context and historical signal similarity.
Supported a UT Neurology long-COVID IVIG therapy study with reproducible cytokine analysis and publication-oriented figure generation.

experience.title: Software Engineer, AI/ML Ops
bullets: Developed and deployed custom AI/ML APIs for training, inference, and autonomous insights.
Built full-stack data pipelines from backend services through AWS to Snowflake, Sigma dashboards, and custom UI.
Modernized platforms by integrating Vue.js with Django templates, improving UX and maintainability.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Provided debugging and architectural review for student and internal projects across unfamiliar codebases.
Dockerized internal tooling and implemented caching layers using the Google Sheets API.

experience.title: Software Engineering Writer
bullets: Created programming articles for an online publication with more than 2 million monthly views.

projects.name: AdBrain
projects.description: A production-grade AI/ML microservice architecture for automated model training, inference, forecasting, and contextual marketing intelligence.
bullets: Implemented automated time series forecasting pipelines and cognitive AI agent architecture.
Built scalable endpoints for training, testing, and managing deep learning and decision-tree models.
Designed flexible neural networks and custom estimators for long-running hyperparameter tuning.
Developed Seq2Seq NLP networks and retrieval-enhanced pipelines for contextual marketing insights.

projects.name: MarketMaven
projects.description: A private vector-based finance intelligence system for identifying high-confidence breakout setups using feature fingerprints, ensemble scoring, probabilistic analogs, and applied physics concepts from fluid dynamics.
bullets: Built a vector-based system to identify breakout setups using feature fingerprints, ensemble scoring, and market-flow analogs inspired by fluid dynamics.
Used LLMs to interpret public sentiment only after qualifying it against deep embedding-space context and historical signal neighborhoods.
Modeled investment confidence using probabilistic analogs, real-time separability metrics, and regime-aware signal comparison.

projects.name: UT IVIG Therapy Study
projects.description: A University of Texas Neurology collaboration analyzing long-COVID IVIG therapy response patterns through cytokine profiles, questionnaire timing, and reproducible figure-generation workflows.
bullets: Linked patient-reported response timing with multiplex cytokine measurements to compare early and late IVIG treatment responders.
Built reproducible analysis code for patient-level PCA, cytokine loadings, PDGF-AA visualization, and responder-group heatmaps.
Prepared publication-oriented PNG/PDF figure outputs and a clean handoff structure for UT Neurology collaborators.

certifications: AWS Cloud Practitioner (Solutions Architect in progress)

education.details: B.A. Psychology + Premedical Biochemistry major, Jul 2011 - May 2014
